27					t.Fatalf("executor must not run for drained queue")
28					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
29				}),
30			}
31			result, exitReason, err := runStopConditionCase(t, worker, context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
32				PollInterval: 0,
33			})
34			require.NoError(t, err)
35			require.NotNil(t, result)

48						SessionID: "sess-once-stop",

115	// TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterImplementerCostStopsBeforeNextClaim verifies
116	// that after an implementer attempt's cost trips the cap, the BudgetStop
117	// callback fires at the top of the next loop iteration and prevents the next
118	// bead from being claimed. This is the AC3 (ddx-b1cf1f6b) coverage path.
119	func TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterImplementerCostStopsBeforeNextClaim(t *testing.T) {
120		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
121		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
122		tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(5.0, func(string) bool { return true })
123	
124		var execCalls int32
125		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
126			Store: store,
127			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
128				atomic.AddInt32(&execCalls, 1)
129				// Accumulate $6 — trips the $5 cap; mirrors accumulateBilledCost.
130				tracker.Add("openrouter", 6.0)
131				return ExecuteBeadReport{
132					BeadID:    beadID,
133					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
134					SessionID: "sess-budget-impl",
135					ResultRev: "abc123",
136				}, nil
137			}),
138		}
139	
140		result, exitReason, err := runStopConditionCase(t, worker, context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
141			BudgetStop: func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
142				detail, tripped := tracker.Tripped()
143				if !tripped {
144					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
145				}
146				return ExecuteBeadReport{
147					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
148					Detail: detail,
149				}, true
150			},
151			PollInterval: 0,
152		})
153		require.NoError(t, err)
154		require.NotNil(t, result)
155		assert.Equal(t, "budget", exitReason)
156		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls), "first bead must be attempted")
157		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "second bead must not be claimed after budget stop")
158	}
159	
160	// TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose verifies that when
161	// reviewer cost trips the budget cap in the APPROVE path, RunPostMergeReview
162	// records the review events but does NOT call CloseWithEvidence and returns
163	// Approved=false. This is the AC2 (ddx-b1cf1f6b) coverage path.
164	func TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose(t *testing.T) {
165		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
166		// Pre-charge $4 of $5 cap; reviewer will add $1.5, tripping the cap.
167		tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(5.0, func(string) bool { return true })
168		tracker.Add("openrouter", 4.0)
169	
170		reviewer := makeReviewerWithCost(VerdictApprove, "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nAll good.", 1.5)
171		out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
172			Bead: *first,
173			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
174				BeadID:    first.ID,
175				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
176				SessionID: "sess-budget-review",
177				ResultRev: "deadcafe",
178				CostUSD:   4.0,
179			},
180			Reviewer:      reviewer,
181			Store:         store,
182			ProjectRoot:   t.TempDir(),
183			Rcfg:          config.NewTestConfigForLoop(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"})),
184			Now:           time.Now,
185			Assignee:      "worker",
186			ReviewCostCap: tracker,
187		})
188	
189		require.False(t, out.Approved, "APPROVE whose reviewer cost trips the budget cap must not close the bead")
190		require.NoError(t, out.StoreErr)
191	
192		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
193		require.NoError(t, err)
194		require.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "bead must remain open when budget is exceeded by reviewer cost")
195	
196		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
197		require.NoError(t, err)
198		var foundApprove, foundDeferred bool
199		for _, ev := range events {
200			if ev.Kind == "review" && ev.Summary == "APPROVE" {
201				foundApprove = true
202			}
203			if ev.Kind == "review-cost-deferred" {
204				foundDeferred = true
205			}
206		}
207		assert.True(t, foundApprove, "APPROVE review event must be recorded even when budget exceeded")
208		assert.True(t, foundDeferred, "review-cost-deferred event must be recorded when budget exceeded by reviewer cost")
209	}
210	
211	func runStopConditionCase(t *testing.T, worker *ExecuteBeadWorker, ctx context.Context, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, string, error) {
212		t.Helper()
213	
214		var sink bytes.Buffer
215		runtime.EventSink = &sink
216		runtime.SessionID = "sess-stop-condition"
217		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
218		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
219		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, runtime)
220		exitReason := loopEndExitReason(t, sink.String())
221		return result, exitReason, err
222	}
223	
224	func loopEndExitReason(t *testing.T, raw string) string {
225		t.Helper()
226		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
227			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
228				continue
229			}
230			var entry map[string]any
231			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
232			if entry["type"] != "loop.end" {
233				continue
234			}
235			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
236			require.True(t, ok, "loop.end data missing")
237			reason, _ := data["exit_reason"].(string)
238			return reason
239		}
240		require.Fail(t, "loop.end event not found", raw)
241		return ""
242	}

752	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies that REQUEST_CHANGES
753	// reopens the bead for the next attempt but does not cause an infinite loop
754	// within a single worker.Run call. The bead is visited once, the reviewer
755	// returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the bead is reopened — but the "attempted" map
756	// prevents it from being picked up again in the same run.
757	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {

1	package work
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"time"
6	)
7	
8	// StopCondition is the typed work-drain terminal decision vocabulary. Keep
9	// these values stable; evidence and operator surfaces derive from them.
10	type StopCondition string
11	
12	const (
13		StopConditionDrained    StopCondition = "Drained"
14		StopConditionOnce       StopCondition = "Once"
15		StopConditionSignal     StopCondition = "Signal"
16		StopConditionBudget     StopCondition = "Budget"
17		StopConditionNoProgress StopCondition = "NoProgress"
18		StopConditionBlocked    StopCondition = "Blocked"
19	)
20	
21	// StopInput describes one possible terminal drain decision point.
22	type StopInput struct {
23		ContextErr   error
24		NoReadyWork  bool
25		Once         bool
26		PollInterval time.Duration
27		Budget       bool
28		NoProgress   bool
29		Blocked      bool
30	}
31	
32	// StopDecision is the classified terminal decision plus the legacy-compatible
33	// loop.end exit_reason string.
34	type StopDecision struct {
35		Condition  StopCondition
36		ExitReason string
37	}
38	
39	// ClassifyStop maps scattered drain-loop terminal checks to a single typed
40	// vocabulary while preserving existing exit_reason strings.
41	func ClassifyStop(in StopInput) (StopDecision, bool) {
42		switch {
43		case in.ContextErr != nil:
44			return StopDecision{
45				Condition:  StopConditionSignal,
46				ExitReason: signalExitReason(in.ContextErr),
47			}, true
48		case in.Budget:
49			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBudget, ExitReason: "budget"}, true
50		case in.NoProgress:
51			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionNoProgress, ExitReason: "no_progress"}, true
52		case in.Blocked:
53			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
54		case in.Once:
55			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
56		case in.NoReadyWork && in.PollInterval <= 0:
57			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "explicit_poll_zero"}, true
58		default:
59			return StopDecision{}, false
60		}
61	}
62	
63	func signalExitReason(err error) string {
64		switch err {
65		case context.Canceled:
66			return "sigint"
67		case context.DeadlineExceeded:
68			return "sigterm"
69		default:
70			return "context_cancelled"
71		}
72	}

116	// that after an implementer attempt's cost trips the cap, the BudgetStop
117	// callback fires at the top of the next loop iteration and prevents the next

119	func TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterImplementerCostStopsBeforeNextClaim(t *testing.T) {

127			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {

129				// Accumulate $6 — trips the $5 cap; mirrors accumulateBilledCost.
130				tracker.Add("openrouter", 6.0)
131				return ExecuteBeadReport{

133					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
134					SessionID: "sess-budget-impl",

140		result, exitReason, err := runStopConditionCase(t, worker, context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
141			BudgetStop: func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
142				detail, tripped := tracker.Tripped()
143				if !tripped {
144					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
145				}
146				return ExecuteBeadReport{
147					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
148					Detail: detail,
149				}, true
150			},
151			PollInterval: 0,
152		})
153		require.NoError(t, err)
154		require.NotNil(t, result)
155		assert.Equal(t, "budget", exitReason)
156		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls), "first bead must be attempted")

160	// TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose verifies that when
161	// reviewer cost trips the budget cap in the APPROVE path, RunPostMergeReview
162	// records the review events but does NOT call CloseWithEvidence and returns
163	// Approved=false. This is the AC2 (ddx-b1cf1f6b) coverage path.
164	func TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose(t *testing.T) {
165		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
166		// Pre-charge $4 of $5 cap; reviewer will add $1.5, tripping the cap.
167		tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(5.0, func(string) bool { return true })
168		tracker.Add("openrouter", 4.0)
169	
170		reviewer := makeReviewerWithCost(VerdictApprove, "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nAll good.", 1.5)
171		out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
172			Bead: *first,
173			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
174				BeadID:    first.ID,
175				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
176				SessionID: "sess-budget-review",
177				ResultRev: "deadcafe",
178				CostUSD:   4.0,
179			},
180			Reviewer:      reviewer,
181			Store:         store,
182			ProjectRoot:   t.TempDir(),
183			Rcfg:          config.NewTestConfigForLoop(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"})),
184			Now:           time.Now,
185			Assignee:      "worker",
186			ReviewCostCap: tracker,
187		})
188	
189		require.False(t, out.Approved, "APPROVE whose reviewer cost trips the budget cap must not close the bead")
190		require.NoError(t, out.StoreErr)
191	
192		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
193		require.NoError(t, err)
194		require.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "bead must remain open when budget is exceeded by reviewer cost")
195	
196		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
197		require.NoError(t, err)
198		var foundApprove, foundDeferred bool
199		for _, ev := range events {
200			if ev.Kind == "review" && ev.Summary == "APPROVE" {
201				foundApprove = true
202			}
203			if ev.Kind == "review-cost-deferred" {
204				foundDeferred = true
205			}
206		}
207		assert.True(t, foundApprove, "APPROVE review event must be recorded even when budget exceeded")
208		assert.True(t, foundDeferred, "review-cost-deferred event must be recorded when budget exceeded by reviewer cost")
209	}

211	func runStopConditionCase(t *testing.T, worker *ExecuteBeadWorker, ctx context.Context, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, string, error) {
212		t.Helper()
213	
214		var sink bytes.Buffer
215		runtime.EventSink = &sink
216		runtime.SessionID = "sess-stop-condition"
217		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
218		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
219		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, runtime)
220		exitReason := loopEndExitReason(t, sink.String())
221		return result, exitReason, err
222	}
223	
224	func loopEndExitReason(t *testing.T, raw string) string {
225		t.Helper()
226		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
227			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
228				continue
229			}
230			var entry map[string]any
231			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
232			if entry["type"] != "loop.end" {
233				continue
234			}
235			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
236			require.True(t, ok, "loop.end data missing")
237			reason, _ := data["exit_reason"].(string)
238			return reason
239		}
240		require.Fail(t, "loop.end event not found", raw)
241		return ""
242	}
